Title: Public S3 Bucket with Sensitive Data
Description: An S3 bucket containing sensitive customer data is publicly accessible without authentication.
Remediation: Enable S3 Block Public Access settings and restrict bucket policy to authorized principals only.

Title: Exposed Kubernetes API Server
Description: The Kubernetes API server is accessible from the internet without IP allowlisting.
Remediation: Restrict API server access using network policies and IP allowlisting. Enable RBAC.

Title: Container Running as Root
Description: A production container is running as root which increases the blast radius of a compromise.
Remediation: Add a non-root USER directive in the Dockerfile and set runAsNonRoot in the pod security context.

Title: Outdated Node.js with Known CVEs
Description: Node.js 14.x is end-of-life and contains multiple known vulnerabilities.
Remediation: Upgrade to Node.js 20 LTS or later.

Title: Unencrypted EBS Volume
Description: An EBS volume attached to a database server is not encrypted at rest.
Remediation: Enable EBS encryption using AWS KMS for all volumes containing sensitive data.

Title: Overly Permissive IAM Role
Description: An IAM role attached to a Lambda function has wildcard (*) permissions on all S3 actions.
Remediation: Apply least privilege by restricting IAM role permissions to only required actions and resources.

Title: Missing MFA on Privileged IAM Users
Description: Privileged IAM users do not have MFA enforced.
Remediation: Enable MFA for all IAM users with console access especially those with admin privileges.

Title: Security Group Allows Unrestricted SSH
Description: A security group allows inbound SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0.
Remediation: Restrict SSH access to specific trusted IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager.

Title: CloudTrail Logging Disabled in Region
Description: CloudTrail is not enabled in us-west-2 leaving API activity unlogged.
Remediation: Enable CloudTrail in all regions and configure log file validation and S3 bucket notifications.

Title: Unused Access Keys Older Than 90 Days
Description: An IAM user has access keys that have not been used in over 90 days.
Remediation: Rotate or deactivate unused access keys to reduce the attack surface.
